description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the python:3.9 Docker image globally, with a pip cache at .cache/pip and variables PIP_CACHE_DIR, TERM, TERMINFO, TMPDIR set as shown. In the tests stage add a job named Python:3.9 that runs the global before_script (apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip python3-venv; pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel green twine; conditional sed commands to set version from CI_JOB_ID or CI_COMMIT_TAG) then executes mkdir $TMPDIR, make install-python, make prepare, and make tests, and captures coverage using the regex /^TOTAL.+?(\d+\%)$/. In the publish stage add a deploy_production job that uses the python:latest image, runs a before_script pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel twine, runs only on tag pipelines, and executes mkdir $TMPDIR, python setup.py sdist, and twine upload --repository-url $PRODUCTION_REPOSITORY_URL dist/* with TERM, TWINE_PASSWORD and TWINE_USERNAME variables. Also in the publish stage add a deploy_staging job that also uses python:latest, runs the same before_script, runs on all branches except tags, and executes mkdir $TMPDIR, python setup.py sdist, and twine upload --repository-url $STAGING_REPOSITORY_URL dist/* with TERM, TWINE_PASSWORD and TWINE_USERNAME variables. Finally, in the documentations stage add a documentations job that runs on all branches except tags, inherits the global before_script, and runs mkdir $TMPDIR, make install-python, make prepare, and make docs.
